# growthbook_custom_field (Resource)

A GrowthBook custom field. Custom fields add structured metadata to features and experiments.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "growthbook_custom_field" "jira_ticket" {
  id       = "jira-ticket"
  name     = "Jira ticket"
  type     = "text"
  required = false
  sections = ["feature", "experiment"]
}

resource "growthbook_custom_field" "risk_level" {
  id       = "risk-level"
  name     = "Risk level"
  type     = "enum"
  values   = "low,medium,high"
  required = true
  sections = ["experiment"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Unique key for the custom field. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) Display name of the custom field.
- `required` (Boolean) Whether a value must be provided for this field.
- `sections` (List of String) Object types this field applies to: `feature` and/or `experiment`.
- `type` (String) Value type of the custom field: `text`, `textarea`, `markdown`, `enum`, `multiselect`, `url`, `number`, `boolean`, `date`, or `datetime`. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the custom field is active.
- `default_value` (String) JSON-encoded default value. Encodes whichever shape the field type accepts (e.g. `"text"`, `42`, `true`, or `["a","b"]`).
- `description` (String) Optional description of the custom field.
- `placeholder` (String) Placeholder text shown in the input.
- `projects` (List of String) Project IDs this field applies to. Empty means all projects.
- `values` (String) Comma-separated list of allowed values for `enum`/`multiselect` types.

### Read-Only

- `creator` (String) User ID of the field creator.
- `date_created` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC3339).
- `date_updated` (String) Last update timestamp (RFC3339).
